title: Africa Unpacked: How the continent is navigating an oil supply crisis
gist: This is a promotional preview of an Oxford Economics Africa 'Africa Unpacked' briefing session on how the continent is navigating the oil supply repercussions of the Middle East conflict; it teases commodity price assumptions for Brent crude and fertiliser, GDP forecast revisions since February for major African markets, and recent inflation figures, but contains no specific data in the text.

insights.claim: Oxford Economics Africa has adjusted its GDP growth forecasts for several major African markets since February in response to the Middle East conflict's effects.
insights.speaker: Sanele Mjikane
insights.so_what: Signals downward-or-revised African growth expectations tied to the oil shock, relevant for frontier-market and commodity exposure.
insights.quote: Show how our GDP growth forecasts have been adjusted since February for several major African markets.

insights.claim: Oxford Economics frames the African outlook around its global commodity price assumptions for Brent crude and fertiliser amid the oil supply crisis.
insights.speaker: Sanele Mjikane
insights.so_what: Brent and fertiliser price paths are the key macro inputs for African importers' inflation and fiscal balances.
insights.quote: Our global commodity price assumptions for Brent crude and fertiliser.

insights.claim: Oxford Economics highlights recent African inflation figures and the limited measures governments can take to placate price pressures.
insights.speaker: Sanele Mjikane
insights.so_what: Constrained policy options against imported inflation point to persistent price pressure risk for African sovereigns.
insights.quote: Discuss the most recent inflation figures and what some governments can do to placate price pressures.
